steps.title: Identify slope and y-intercept
steps.content: Slope-intercept form is $y = mx + b$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the $y$-intercept. The slope is given as $m = 7$. The point $(0, 5)$ has $x = 0$, which means it is the $y$-intercept, so $b = 5$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $y = -5x + 7$: Slope is $-5$, not $7$. Eliminated.
  • B) $y = -5x - 7$: Slope is $-5$ and $y$-intercept is $-7$. Neither matches. Eliminated.
  • C) $y = 7x + 5$: Slope $7$ and $y$-intercept $5$. Matches.
  • D) $y = 7x - 5$: Slope $7$ is right, but $y$-intercept is $-5$, not $5$. Eliminated.
That confirms C is correct.
